title: Diff file path normalization collapses real top-level a/ and b/ paths
reasoning: The same helper strips `a/` or `b/` from both parsed diff names and application-level file paths passed via `scrollToFilePath`. For a legitimate repository path like `a/config.ts` or `b/config.ts`, this normalizes it to `config.ts`. That can make jump-to-file fail or scroll to the wrong diff when both `config.ts` and `a/config.ts` are present, and it can also make rendered `data-diff-file-path` values ambiguous. Diff prefix stripping should only happen on raw git header paths known to include the synthetic `a/` or `b/` side prefix, not on already-normalized repo paths.
recommendation: Keep application file paths unchanged, and normalize only raw diff header paths with side-prefix context. If `@pierre/diffs` already returns repo-relative names, remove `stripDiffPathPrefix` from `resolveFileDiffPath` and `scrollToFilePath`; otherwise track whether the value came from an `a/` or `b/` header before stripping.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The included tests exercise fix-attempt file buttons but do not render DiffViewer or verify jumping to paths under real top-level `a/` or `b/` directories.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a DiffViewer/extract path test using a diff containing both `foo.ts` and `a/foo.ts`, then trigger a jump to `a/foo.ts` and assert the matching rendered file wrapper is targeted distinctly from `foo.ts`.
minimumFixScope: src/renderer/src/components/DiffViewer.tsx and a focused renderer/unit test for diff path normalization.
